title: KX-05: Companion layer — onboarding import, self-repairs, builder control, experts, chat polish
description: DRAFT for Jacob's review — NOT yet applied to the Builder queue. Derived 2026-07-23 from agent dogfooding (fresh-profile browser walkthrough), chat-history mining (the 'start buidery' session), and Jacob's direction: he is not a coder, everything must be operable from the UI, onboarding must actually onboard, and experts over the local library must be easy to reach. Gaps confirmed NOT covered by KX-01..KX-04 or closeout-v2: onboarding that imports history (packet 024's extractor + idea-mine REST exist but have no UI and zero imported rows), self-diagnosis with fix actions (doctor exists CLI-only; the UI shows raw jargon banners), builder queue control beyond B7/B8's next-packet gate (pause/resume/cancel/cleanup are CLI-only; 'needs a decision' cards have no decision buttons), experts surfacing (219 books tagged by expert in data/books_manifest.json, council + knowledge routes exist, no UI), and a chat polish sweep (junk task cards eat the thread, memory over-recall, status-strip flapping, CLI copy in user-facing text). Sequencing: KX-05-03 depends on closeout-v2 B7/B8 landing (authority gate pattern); KX-05-02 and KX-05-05 are independent quick wins; KX-05-01 pairs with KX-02 approval cards for the import review; KX-05-04 pairs with KX-02 tool cards for citations.

packets.title: Onboarding that actually onboards: persist to gateway, import chat history
packets.objective: Today onboarding collects a name and theme into localStorage, then does nothing visible (the name appears nowhere) and re-runs on every new origin/device because completion is browser-local. Fix in three moves. (1) Persist onboarding completion, preferred name, and theme to the gateway app_settings table (it already stores active_project_id); the UI reads/writes through /proxy so any browser, PWA, or phone sees the same state. (2) Use the preferred name: greeting on Home, and pass it into the chat system context so Kitty addresses Jacob by name. (3) Add an optional import step to onboarding: point at a ChatGPT conversations.json export, Claude export, or a folder of transcripts; run the EXISTING scripts/curation/extract_chat_goldmine.py pipeline; show the found items in a review list backed by the EXISTING /idea-mine/review + PATCH endpoints (idea_mine_items is empty today); approved items enter memory per the packet 024 phase 2-3 rules. No new extractor is built; this is wiring and UI only.
acceptance_criteria: Completing onboarding on one browser means no other origin/device shows onboarding again (verified by fresh-profile check against the same gateway)
Home greets Jacob by name within one render of load, and chat context includes the preferred name
Import wizard accepts a ChatGPT conversations.json and lists extracted items with approve/skip per item
Approved items are queryable in chat within one turn (memory evidence line shows the source)
Skipped items are remembered as skipped and not re-offered on the next import
make ui-test passes; npm run build passes; python3.12 -m pytest tests/ -q passes

packets.title: Repairs: Kitty tells Jacob what is wrong in plain English, with fix buttons
packets.objective: Pattern: Home Assistant Repairs. Today system state is scattered as jargon ('partial packet records', 'POST /state/snapshot', './kitty up starts it', a flickering 'gateway offline'). Build one repairs feed: a Home card plus a chat intent ('kitty, what's wrong?') backed by the same gateway endpoint. The endpoint composes: kitty doctor checks, litellm/gateway reachability, builder queue anomalies (stale leases, exhausted budgets, zombie tasks), and disk/backup age (the queue backup warning). Each issue renders as: plain-English sentence ('the model router stopped answering 4 minutes ago'), severity, and where safe a fix button. Fix buttons go through the EXISTING action queue (gateway/routes/actions.py propose/approve/execute) so T2 approval rules stay enforced — e.g. 'restart the router', 'release the stuck builder lease', 'cancel the 11 dead tasks'. Destructive or paid actions remain approval-gated. Forbidden: raw CLI commands, file paths, or API routes in user-facing copy — encode both as snapshot tests.
acceptance_criteria: One /repairs gateway endpoint returns issues with plain-English titles, severity, and optional action ids
Home shows a repairs card; when empty it says so in one warm line, not a grid of empty boxes
Asking 'what's wrong' in chat returns the same feed as cards with working fix buttons
A fix button executes through the action queue with the correct tier gate (verified by a test that a T2 action demands approval)
No user-facing string contains './kitty', 'POST /', 'curl', or a filesystem path (snapshot test)
make ui-test passes; npm run build passes; python3.12 -m pytest tests/ -q passes

packets.title: Builder control deck: run, pause, resume, cancel, cleanup, and real decision cards
packets.objective: Jacob: 'in the kittybuilder plane there is nothing for me to do.' The surface is read-only by design and the one 'needs a decision' card has no buttons. Extend closeout-v2 B7/B8's authority-gate pattern into a full control deck on the Builder surface. Actions (all through the action queue with T2 gates, never direct DB writes): run next eligible packet, pause/resume an initiative, operator-cancel a task, and 'clean up dead work' (the exact zombie sweep performed by hand on 2026-07-23: cancel tasks of superseded initiatives with a summary of what will be cancelled before confirm). needs_decision pauses render as decision cards with the judgment question restated in plain English and approve/deny/edit choices. Also fix the attention projection: operator-cancelled tasks must not count as 'needs attention' (today cancelling the 11 zombies RAISED the counter to 24). Plain-English layer: 'attempt 2 · scope failure' becomes 'the worker went outside its allowed files — tap to see what changed'.
acceptance_criteria: Every control action round-trips through the action queue with tier enforcement (tests prove no direct mutation path)
A paused initiative can be resumed and a queued task cancelled from the UI without touching the CLI
The cleanup action previews the exact tasks it will cancel and requires confirm
needs-decision initiatives render actionable cards; deciding one unblocks the queue end-to-end in the smoke run
packetNeedsAttention (or its successor) excludes operator-cancelled tasks; unit test covers the 2026-07-23 zombie sweep case
make ui-test passes; npm run build passes; python3.12 -m pytest tests/ -q passes

packets.title: Experts shelf: talk to the library, with citations
packets.objective: The backend already has the ingredients: 219 books tagged by expert in data/books_manifest.json, chroma knowledge collections, /ask, /knowledge, and the council router. Nothing in the UI exposes them. Build the thinnest honest version: an Experts strip on Home (one card per expert with 'what I know' in one line, derived from the manifest) and expert addressing in chat: an expert chip picker in the input bar (and '@builder why is my...' prefix) that routes the turn through the expert's filtered collection and persona prompt, then renders answers with book/passage citations as collapsible cards (KX-02 tool-card pattern). Scope discipline: experts are read-only Q&A over the existing collections — no new ingestion, no fine-tuning, no per-expert memory in this packet. If an expert's collection is empty, the card says what to add and links to the import flow from KX-05-01.
acceptance_criteria: Home shows an experts strip generated from books_manifest (no hardcoded expert list)
Selecting an expert in chat visibly scopes the turn; the answer carries at least one citation card naming the source book
Citation cards expand to the passage and source path
An expert with an empty collection shows the empty state and import link instead of a broken answer
make ui-test passes; npm run build passes; python3.12 -m pytest tests/ -q passes

packets.title: Chat polish sweep: kill the junk, tune recall, stop the flapping
packets.objective: Five small correctness fixes found by dogfooding on 2026-07-23, bundled because each is a few lines plus a test. (1) ActiveTaskCards: never render gateway task-queue items titled like test data, cap at 3 cards, and collapse behind 'N more' — today nine 'task test' rows consume the entire thread area on a fresh chat. (2) Memory evidence: suppress the 'kitty remembered N things' line when retrieval confidence is low or the turn is smalltalk — 13 items on 'what can you do' is noise. (3) Status strip: require 3 consecutive failed polls before showing 'gateway offline' — a single failed poll currently flips the whole header red while chat still works. (4) Fix 'last session: Next session' — the resume card renders the STATE.md section heading as the session title. (5) Purge remaining CLI/API copy from user-facing strings surfaced during the sweep (same snapshot-test rule as KX-05-02).
acceptance_criteria: A fresh chat shows at most 3 task cards and zero rows matching test-data patterns (unit test with the 2026-07-23 seed data)
Smalltalk turns produce no memory-evidence line; a real recall turn still shows it
Status strip tolerates a single failed probe without changing state (fake-timers test)
Resume card shows the actual next action text, never a raw markdown heading
make ui-test passes; npm run build passes
